entries.title: Android | Do More With Google on Android Phones & Devices
entries.summary:
* Android features intelligent eyewear, including audio glasses coming this fall
* Google Gemini is an AI assistant available on Android devices
* Android XR expands reality beyond screens, including headsets and glasses
* Circle to Search allows users to search with one gesture
* Google Messages brings fun features to chat, including photo remixing
* Android devices require internet connection for certain features
* Gemini mobile app availability varies by device, country, and language
* Android XR and other features have compatibility variations by device and region
* Google AI is integrated into various Android features and apps

entries.source_url: https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Android_(オペレーティングシステム)
entries.title: Android (オペレーティングシステム) - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* AndroidはGoogleが開発したモバイルオペレーティングシステム
* Linuxカーネルとオープンソースソフトウェアがベース
* プログラミング言語: C言語、Java、Kotlin
* OSの系統: Unix系
* カーネル: モノリシックカーネル
* ライセンス: Apache 2.0、GPL v2、LGPL
* プラットフォーム: ARM32、ARM64、MIPS、x86、x64
* アップデート方式: Over The Air (OTA)
* 使用できる言語: 日本語など100言語以上
* 主な競合: iOS、iPadOS、Windows Mobile、Windows Phone、Symbian OSなど
* Android supports multi-touch gestures like swiping and pinching
* Android devices have hardware components like gyroscopes, GPS, and accelerometers
* Android has a virtual keyboard for text input and supports external keyboards via Bluetooth or USB
* Android has a status bar that displays system information and notifications
* Android has a navigation bar with buttons for back, home, and multi-tasking
* Android supports installation of third-party IMEs (Input Method Editors) for text input
* Android has a standard C library called Bionic, which is designed for limited memory and processing capacity
* Android's kernel is based on Linux
* Android supports various network technologies like GSM, UMTS, CDMA2000, EV-DO, and LTE
* Android has a package manager called Google Play that allows users to download and install apps
* AndroidはglibcではなくBionicライブラリを使用
* BionicはFreeBSD、NetBSD、OpenBSDのコードを組み合わせて作成
* AndroidはLinuxカーネルに独自パッチを当てたAndroid共通カーネル（ACK）を使用
* サポートしているCPUはARM（ARMv5以降）、MIPS、x86
* Android Runtime（ART）が標準の仮想マシン
* Dalvik仮想マシンはメモリ消費を低く抑えた設計
* SQLiteがデータ保存用に組み込まれている
* Media Frameworkにより、H.263、H.264、H.265、MPEG-4 SPなどの映像と音声形式に対応
* プログラミング言語はC言語、C++、Rust（2021年から新規コードのデ

  * Android 16 (2025)

  * Android 14 (2023)

  * Android 12 (2021)

* Current dominant Android versions are Android 15 and 14.
* Android 16 is the latest version, released in June 2025.

* Android versions prior to 10 have official dessert-themed code names, e.g., KitKat, Lollipop, Marshmallow.

entries.title: Flutter - Build apps for any screen
entries.summary:
* Flutter is an open-source framework for building multi-platform applications.
* Flutter code compiles to ARM or Intel machine code and JavaScript for fast performance.
* Hot Reload allows for quick build and iteration with almost instant code updates.
* Flutter supports deployment to multiple devices from a single codebase: mobile, web, desktop, and embedded devices.
* Flutter is powered by the Dart language, optimized for fast apps on any platform.
* Automated testing and developer tooling are available for building production-quality apps.
* Flutter has seamless integration with Google services like Firebase, Google Ads, and Google Maps.
* The framework is supported by Google and maintained by a global community of developers.
* Flutter allows for customized, adaptive designs with control over every pixel.
* Dart packages are available on pub.dev for extending Flutter functionality.
* Flutter is a framework for building apps
* Supports building apps for any screen
* Offers instant access to its power
* Installation is available
* Documentation is provided
* Getting started is a straightforward process
* Flutter has a dedicated website (flutter.dev) 
* Offers a newsletter for updates and information

entries.title: Flutter Tutorial - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* Flutter is Google's mobile SDK for building native Android and iOS apps from a single codebase.
* Flutter uses the Dart programming language and provides ready-to-use widgets for designing user interfaces.
* Hot reload is a special feature of Flutter that allows developers to see changes instantly as they code.
* Flutter apps are built using widgets, which are the blocks that make up the user interface.
* Prerequisites for learning Flutter include a strong grasp of Dart programming, familiarity with Android Studio, and knowledge of web scripting languages like HTML, JavaScript, and CSS.
* Flutter provides a rich library of built-in widgets, including buttons, text fields, layouts, and animations.
* Widgets in Flutter can be either stateful or stateless, with stateful widgets having their own state and stateless widgets relying on their parent widget for state.
* The MaterialApp class is a top-level widget in Flutter that provides a basic material design visual layout structure.
* Flutter supports development on multiple platforms, including Android, iOS, web, and desktop.
* Key Flutter widgets include AppBar, FloatingActionButton, BottomNavigationBar, Drawer, Container, and ListView.
* Flutter provides pre-built UI components like buttons, text fields, and sliders.
* Icon, Expanded, and Checkbox are some of the available widget classes in Flutter.
* Flutter supports various layout components like Carousel Slider, Staggered Grid View, and Tabs.
* Customizable fonts, themes,

entries.title: Kotlin Programming Language
entries.summary:
* Kotlin is a multiplatform programming language.
* It supports cross-platform development without compromising performance, UX, or code quality.
* Kotlin is used for Android, backend, and AI development.
* It is an object-oriented and functional language.
* Kotlin has built-in support for asynchronous programming.
* The language is ideal for tests and has a concise syntax.
* Kotlin is developed by JetBrains.
* It has been the official language of Android development since 2019.
* Kotlin supports integration with Spring and Ktor for backend development.
* The language has a growing real-world impact, including use in AI-driven workflows and multiplatform development.
* Kotlin has a Security Support Policy for its Standard Library.
* Official Kotlin extension for Visual Studio Code is available in Alpha.
* Kotlin prioritizes developer convenience and productivity over academic purity.
* Kotlin supports asynchronous, object-oriented, and functional programming.
* Kotlin is ideal for tests and has a simple syntax.
* Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) allows for shared codebase across platforms.
* Kotlin is used for server-side development by companies like AWS and Adobe.
* Kotlin's concise syntax and async capabilities improve productivity.
* Kotlin has interoperability with Java.
* Kotlin Multiplatform enables sharing of over 80% of logic across iOS and Android.
* Kotlin allows sharing over 80% of logic across iOS and Android platforms.
* Kotlin Multiplatform simplifies cross-platform

entries.title: Kotlin - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* Kotlin is a multi-paradigm language: object-oriented, functional, imperative, block structured, generic, reflective, concurrent.
* Typing discipline: inferred, static, strong.
* Platforms: Android, JVM, iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS, macOS, Linux, Windows, JavaScript, WebAssembly.
* License: Apache 2.0.
* Filename extensions: .kt, .kts, .kexe, .klib.
* Influenced by: C#, Eiffel, Gosu, Groovy, Java, JavaScript, ML, Scala.
* Compiles to: JVM, JavaScript, native code via LLVM.
* Stable release: 2.3.0 (December 2025).
* Supports type inference and interoperability with Java.
* Used for: Android app development, frontend web applications, native iOS apps.
* Kotlin was first committed to the Git repository on 8 November 2010.
* Kotlin is designed to be an industrial-strength object-oriented language, interoperable with Java code.
* Semicolons are optional as statement terminators in Kotlin.
* Variable declarations and parameter lists place the data type after the variable name, separated by a colon.
* Kotlin supports both object-oriented and functional programming.
* Variables can be declared as mutable (var) or immutable (val).
* Functions and methods support default arguments, variable-length argument lists, and named arguments.
* Kotlin 1.3 added support for contracts, inspired by the design-by-contract programming paradigm.
* Kotlin code can be transpiled to JavaScript, enabling interoperability between Kotlin and JavaScript.
* Kotlin 1.0 was released on 15 February 2016, with a

* Kotlin helps boost productivity, developer satisfaction, and code safety.

* Kotlin-friendly API reference documentation is available for Android development.

* Kotlin is an open-source project under the Apache 2.0 license.

entries.title: Kotlin Tutorial - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* Kotlin is a modern programming language created by JetBrains in 2011.
* Kotlin runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and can be compiled to JavaScript or native code.
* Kotlin is an object-oriented language and is fully interoperable with Java code.
* Kotlin is officially supported by Google for Android development.
* Key features of Kotlin include null safety, coroutines, and functional programming.
* Kotlin's code is shorter and clearer than Java, making it easier to understand and work with.
* Kotlin can be used with Java in the same project, making it easy to switch from Java to Kotlin.
* Kotlin has a growing number of libraries, tools, and community resources to support developers.
* Kotlin environment can be set up using Command Line, IntelliJ IDEA, or Android Studio.
* Kotlin's coroutines make it easier to handle background tasks like network requests without blocking the app.
* Kotlin supports type conversion, expressions, statements, and blocks.
* Control flow statements include if-else, while, do-while, and for loops.
* Kotlin has labeled and unlabeled break and continue statements.
* Arrays and strings are supported, with functions for manipulation.
* Functions can have default and named arguments, and support recursion and tail recursion.
* Kotlin supports lambdas, inline functions, infix function notation, and higher-order functions.
* Collections include lists, sets, and maps, with mutable and immutable variants.
* OOPs concepts include classes, objects, inhe

entries.title: Android (operating system) - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* Android devices have a customizable home screen with swipe functionality.
* Third-party apps can re-theme the home screen and mimic other operating systems.
* The status bar displays device information and connectivity, and can be pulled down to reveal notifications.
* Notifications provide timely information about apps and can be expanded for more details.
* Android 4.1 "Jelly Bean" introduced expandable notifications.
* The "All Apps" screen lists installed applications, and the "Recents" screen shows recently used apps.
* Navigation buttons include home, menu/options, back, and search, with some devices using a dedicated search button.
* Android version 10 no longer supports the dedicated option key or menu key.
* Split-screen view is natively supported in stock Android version 7.0 Nougat.
* Split-screen view was removed in Android Go Edition to optimize the user experience for low-end devices.

* QPR2 is a stable release version of Android 16.

entries.title: Designing Multi-Module Android Applications: A Comprehensive Guide
entries.summary:
* Clean Architecture is suitable for multi-module Android applications, separating code into Presentation, Domain, and Data layers.
* Multi-module applications can be divided into App, Feature, Core, and Data modules.
* Feature Modules should be self-contained units of functionality, such as authentication or profile management.
* Core Modules should contain shared utilities, resources, and components used across features.
* Data Modules should implement repositories and data sources.
* MVVM pattern complements multi-module architecture, with each feature module implementing its own MVVM.
* Dependency direction is crucial, with Feature Modules not depending on other Feature Modules.
* Core/Common Modules should have minimal or no dependencies on other modules.
* Data Modules may depend on Core Modules but not on Feature Modules.
* Clear separation of concerns and well-defined interfaces are key to successful multi-module design.
* Each feature module implements its own navigation graph and registers with the main navigation controller.
* Consistent module structure: feature_module/ → api/, impl/, build.gradle, README.md.
* Use Kotlin's visibility modifiers (internal, public) to control module exposure.
* Implement testing strategies: unit tests, integration tests, UI tests, and test doubles.
* Create test doubles (mocks, fakes) for module boundaries to enable isolated testing.
* Document module interactions: dependency graphs, API contracts, data flow diagrams, module respons

entries.title: Ithy - Best Practices for Android Multi-Module Architecture
entries.summary:
* Modular Android app architecture helps scale and maintain large projects.
* Multi-module architecture separates features into independent modules.
* Each module should have its own Gradle build file.
* Domain module should not depend on data module, but rather the other way around.
* Use a shared or common module for shared code between features.
* Clean architecture principles can be applied to multi-module architecture.
* Navigation between modules should be handled using a navigation component.
* Use a consistent package structure for multi-module projects.
* Gradle files can be shared between modules using a common gradle file.
* Modularization improves maintainability, scalability, and testability of Android apps.

entries.title: Android Multi-Module Architecture with Material3 Design System
entries.summary:
* Android multi-module architecture reduces build times by compiling only changed modules.
* Modular structure improves testability by allowing isolated module testing.
* Team development scalability is enhanced by clear module boundaries and separation of concerns.
* Modules can be shared across projects, promoting code reusability.
* Recommended architecture includes app, core, feature, and build-logic modules.
* Core module contains shared utilities, networking, and storage.
* Feature modules depend on core, but core cannot depend on features to prevent circular dependencies.
* Convention Plugins define shared configurations to eliminate repetition.
* Dependency direction follows the "Golden Rule": feature modules → core, but core → feature modules is not allowed.
* Module responsibilities include: app (entry point), core-common (utilities), core-network (HTTP client), core-database (Room database).

entries.title: Hilt Dependency Injection for JetPackCompose. Minimum knowledge …
entries.summary:
* Hilt uses `@Singleton` to ensure a single instance of an object throughout the application.
* `@Provides` marks methods that create injectable objects.
* `@InstallIn` determines the scope and lifecycle of a module's provided objects.
* Modules can depend on other modules using Hilt's dependency injection mechanism.
* Use cases (e.g. `GetAllContactsUseCase`, `GetContactByIdUseCase`) encapsulate specific business logic.
* Use cases promote cleaner separation of concerns, reduced code duplication, and improved maintainability.
* `@Inject` constructor is used to inject dependencies into use cases.
* Hilt supports modular and organized code structures through module dependencies.
* Use cases can be used for data validation (e.g. `DataValidUseCase`) and data operations (e.g. `AddContactUseCase`).
* Hilt is used for dependency injection in Jetpack Compose.
* Use cases (e.g. `AddContactUseCase`, `UpdateContactUseCase`, `DeleteContactUseCase`) rely on Hilt for dependency injection.
* `@Inject` constructor is used to inject dependencies into use cases and ViewModels.
* `@HiltViewModel` annotation is used to enable Hilt dependency injection for ViewModels.
* `hiltViewModel()` function is used to create a ViewModel instance with Hilt.
* Hilt automatically creates and injects dependencies for ViewModels.
* ViewModels (e.g. `ContactsViewModel`) can be used in composables without manual instantiation.
* Hilt handles creation and injection of dependencies under the hood.
* Same dependency

entries.title: Kotlin: 5 Advanced Coroutine Patterns You Must Know
entries.summary:
* Kotlin Coroutines revolutionized asynchronous programming on Android.
* Mastering advanced coroutine patterns unlocks cleaner, more efficient, and robust code.
* Custom dispatchers can be created for specialized sequential tasks.
* `Dispatchers.IO`, `Default`, and `Main` are standard dispatchers in Kotlin.
* Custom dispatchers provide granular control over thread pools and concurrency.
* Kotlin's concurrency model is modern and powerful.
* `viewModelScope.launch` and `withContext` are basic coroutine functions.
* Advanced coroutine patterns are necessary for handling complex, real-world scenarios.
* Kotlin remains one of the most loved programming languages, with a 75% developer preference rating.
* Custom dispatchers can limit concurrency for specific operations, such as interacting with singleton resources.

entries.title: Kotlin Coroutines Deep Dive: From Basics to Advanced Patterns
entries.summary:
* Kotlin version required: 1.9+
* Required libraries: kotlinx-coroutines-core, kotlinx-coroutines-android
* AndroidX Lifecycle required for ViewModel and lifecycleScope
* Repository structure includes basics, usecases, advanced, and rxjava folders
* Basics folder covers launch, runBlocking, and viewModelScope
* Usecases folder covers parallel API calls and retry with backoff
* Advanced folder covers Flow, SupervisorScope, and lifecycle-aware coroutine usage
* RxJava folder provides equivalent examples in RxJava and their Coroutine counterparts
* Project can be cloned and opened with Android Studio or IntelliJ for exploration

entries.title: Room Database Complete Guide for Android Developers: Learn Setup ...
entries.summary:
* Room Database is a persistence library for Android that simplifies data handling.
* Room acts as an abstraction layer over SQLite, reducing raw queries and runtime errors.
* Room enforces compile-time validation, making applications more robust and reliable.
* Room has three essential components: entities, data access objects (DAOs), and databases.
* Entities represent the structure of data stored in the database, defining table organization.
* Data access objects (DAOs) serve as an interface between the application and database.
* Room reduces boilerplate code and complex queries associated with traditional SQLite use.
* Room improves overall code quality and maintainability of Android applications.
* Room supports migrations, allowing for changes to the database schema over time.
* Room provides a more structured and organized approach to local data storage in Android.
* Room Database has three main components: entity, data access layer, and database.
* Room integrates with modern Android development tools and architecture components.
* It supports asynchronous operations for smooth performance and responsive user experience.
* Room provides a structured approach to handle database migrations and schema updates.
* It promotes better architectural practices, such as layered architecture and separation of responsibilities.
* Room supports lifecycle-aware components for automatic UI updates.
* It requires careful planning for complex data relationships and migrations in larg

entries.title: Android Room Persistence Library: Complete Guide - daily.dev

entries.title: Buscar personas en Facebook: por nombre, foto, sin registro - CCM
entries.summary:
* Se puede buscar a alguien en Facebook utilizando su correo electrónico en la barra de búsqueda.
* La búsqueda por correo electrónico solo funciona si la persona ha aceptado que su dirección sea visible.
* Se puede buscar a alguien a través de amigos en común o grupos compartidos en Facebook.
* Para buscar por amigos, se debe entrar en el perfil de un amigo en común y revisar su lista de contactos.
* Se puede buscar a alguien en grupos dedicados a centros de estudios, empresas, vecindarios, etc.
* La sección de sugerencias de amistad en Facebook puede ser útil para encontrar a alguien sin conocer su nombre o apellidos.
* Las sugerencias de amistad se basan en la geolocalización y amigos en común.
* La sección de sugerencias de amistad se encuentra en el mismo sitio que las peticiones de amistad pendientes.
* Se puede buscar a alguien en la sección de sugerencias de amistad durante un rato para encontrar coincidencias.
* La búsqueda en Facebook puede ser tediosa, pero no se pierde nada por intentarlo.

entries.title: Best Practices for Android App Performance Optimization
entries.summary:
* Use caching strategies and libraries like Retrofit and OkHttp to minimize redundant network requests.
* Optimize API calls by using HTTP/2, reducing payload size, and implementing pagination.
* Use JobScheduler or WorkManager for deferred tasks to optimize battery life.
* Enable hardware acceleration to offload rendering tasks to the GPU.
* Minimize overdraw using the GPU Overdraw tool in Android Studio.
* Use AsyncTask for simple background tasks and Kotlin Coroutines for complex asynchronous programming.
* Implement thread pools to manage multiple threads efficiently.
* Use Android Profiler to monitor CPU, memory, and network usage.
* Integrate tools like Firebase Performance Monitoring for real-time performance insights.
* Analyze battery usage patterns with Battery Historian to address power wastage.

entries.title: Android App Performance Optimization Guide 2024 - daily.dev
entries.summary:
* Use ConstraintLayout to optimize UI layout performance and reduce layout calculations.
* Set `android:hardwareAccelerated="true"` to minimize over-draw and optimize view hierarchies.
* Leverage RecyclerView to improve list performance and reduce memory usage.
* Use Async Tasks to perform computationally expensive tasks asynchronously and prevent UI blocking.
* Implement Job Scheduling using JobScheduler or WorkManager to optimize resource usage and reduce battery drain.
* Implement caching mechanisms to reduce network requests and load times.
* Target latest Android versions to leverage performance improvements and new features.
* Migrate to AndroidX libraries for optimized and modular implementations of Android APIs.
* Use Android NDK to optimize performance-critical components and take advantage of native code optimizations.
* Utilize Android Profiler to monitor app performance, including CPU usage, memory allocation, and network activity.

entries.title: COMPOSE | English meaning - Cambridge Dictionary
entries.summary:
* Compose is a verb with multiple meanings, including producing art, forming something, becoming calm, and arranging text.
* In music, compose means to produce music, poetry, or formal writing.
* In writing, compose can mean to arrange words, sentences, and pages for printing.
* Compose can also mean to be formed from various things or to be the parts that something is made of.
* The verb compose is used in different contexts, including music, writing, and self-calming.
* Compose is related to other words such as composition, compositional, and composer.
* The verb consist is often used interchangeably with compose, but has slightly different connotations.
* Comprise is another verb that is similar to compose, but is used to describe what something is made of.
* Compose can be used in different tenses, but not in continuous forms.
* The word compose has various synonyms, including orchestrate, reharmonize, and rescore.
* Compose is a verb used to describe creation or formation of something.
* It can mean to produce or create music, poems, or writing.
* Compose can also mean to form or make up something, such as a group or area.
* It is not used in continuous forms.
* Compose is often used to describe the structure or components of something.
* Examples of composition include cells, molecules, and economic landscapes.
* Compose can be used in various contexts, including art, music, and science.
* The verb comprise is often confused with compose, but they have different meaning

entries.title: I tried Android 17, and these are the 5 changes I love most

* Android 17 is the latest version of the Android operating system.
* Google has shifted to a more accelerated release cycle for Android updates.
* Major Android versions are now released mid-year, instead of alongside new Pixel launches.
* Feature drops will follow around the Pixel launch, typically in August or September.

* Android 17 introduces a new feature called App Bubbles, allowing full apps to run in floating windows.
* App Bubbles can be launched by pressing and holding an app icon and tapping the bubble option.
* Multiple App Bubbles can be active at once and moved around together.
* Android 17 improves screen recording features, including options to record device audio, microphone audio, or show touches.
* Screen recording now includes a pill-shaped overlay with a clearly labeled stop button.
* Android 17 is being released with a mid-year stable update and feature drops around the Pixel launch.
* Google Pixel phones and other devices will support App Bubbles out of the box.
* App Bubbles are similar to Facebook Messenger's chat heads, but for full apps.
* Android 17's screen recording feature allows recording of the entire screen or a single app.

* Android 17 introduces a new pill-shaped overlay for screen recording with a stop butt

entries.title: Flutter State Management with Riverpod: A Complete Guide
entries.summary:
* Riverpod's `FutureProvider.family` allows fetching different data based on parameters like user IDs.
* `FutureProvider.family` is useful for API requests, filtering, or item-specific state.
* Scoped providers override the value of a provider in a specific widget subtree.
* Scoped providers are useful for testing, theming, or customizing behavior in specific screens.
* The `ProviderScope` widget is used to override providers locally.
* Family providers are used when state/data depends on parameters like IDs or filters.
* Scoped providers are used when different behaviors/values are needed in specific parts of the app.
* Riverpod allows combining multiple providers to compute new values.
* Computed providers can automatically rebuild the UI when dependencies change.
* Business logic can be kept separate from widgets using Riverpod's reactive provider system.

entries.title: Flutter - Introduction to State Management Using Riverpod
entries.summary:
* Riverpod is a state management library for Flutter.
* State management is crucial for managing app data and UI updates.
* Riverpod uses providers to manage state and notify listeners of changes.
* Providers can be used to manage simple values or complex objects.
* Riverpod supports both simple and complex state management scenarios.
* Flutter apps can use Riverpod to manage state across multiple widgets.
* Riverpod integrates with Flutter's widget tree to update UI automatically.
* Riverpod provides a simple and efficient way to manage app state.
* Riverpod is an alternative to other state management libraries like BLoC and Provider.

entries.title: Flutter State Management with Riverpod: A Complete Guide
entries.summary:
* Riverpod has two main variants: `flutter_riverpod` for Flutter apps and `riverpod` for pure Dart projects.
* Add `flutter_riverpod` dependency to `pubspec.yaml` using `flutter pub add flutter_riverpod`.
* Wrap the app with `ProviderScope` at the root of the widget tree.
* `Provider` is used for read-only values, while `StateProvider` is used for mutable state.
* Define a `Provider` using `Provider<String>((ref) { return 'value'; });`.
* Access a `Provider` value using `ref.watch(provider)`.
* Define a `StateProvider` using `StateProvider<int>((ref) => initialValue)`.
* Read the state of a `StateProvider` using `ref.watch(provider)`.
* Use `ConsumerWidget` to rebuild the widget when the state changes.
* Use `WidgetRef ref` to access providers in the widget tree.

entries.title: Microsoft Edge Browser Policy Documentation ComposeInlineEnabled

* Policy value can be set to Enabled or Disabled.

* Applies to Microsoft Edge version 98 or later.
* Policy can be set using Group Policy or Microsoft Intune.
* Registry key: Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge.
* Policy name: ComposeInlineEnabled.
* Data type: Boolean.

entries.title: CT Scans vs. MRIs: Differences, Benefits, and Risks - Healthline
entries.summary:
* CT scans use ionizing radiation, while MRIs use a strong magnetic field.
* CT scans are faster and better for imaging skeletal structure and general internal body structures.
* MRIs provide more detailed images, especially for soft tissue, ligaments, and organs.
* MRI risks include reactions to implanted metals and complications from dyes in people with kidney disease.
* CT scan risks include harm to unborn babies and potential reactions to dyes.
* MRIs are recommended for herniated disks, torn ligaments, and soft tissue issues.
* CT scans are recommended for general images of internal organs, fractures, or head trauma.
* Both CT scans and MRIs are relatively low risk.
* Claustrophobia and anxiety are potential issues with MRIs.
* People with implants (e.g. pacemakers, artificial joints) should consult a doctor before an MRI.

entries.title: CT Scan vs. MRI: What's the Difference and Which One Is Right ...
entries.summary:
* CT scans use X-rays and are faster, better for bones, bleeding, and trauma.
* MRI scans use magnetic fields with no radiation and are better for soft tissue.
* CT scans have low-moderate radiation, while MRI scans have no radiation.
* CT scans are best for bone, lungs, bleeding, trauma, cancer staging, and emergency imaging.
* MRI scans are best for soft tissue, including brain, spine, joints, muscles, organs, and blood vessels.
* CT scans have excellent bone detail, while MRI scans have excellent soft tissue detail.
* CT scans typically take 5-15 minutes, while MRI scans take 20-60 minutes.
* MRI scans are generally safe in pregnancy, but CT scans are usually avoided.
* CT scans do not have metal implant concerns, while MRI scans require screening for pacemakers and implants.
* MRI scans are typically more expensive than CT scans.

entries.title: GitHub - JetBrains/kotlin: The Kotlin Programming Language.
entries.summary:

* Use `sed` command to delete `components` section from `verification-metadata.xml`.
* Regenerate dependencies with `./gradlew --write-verification-metadata sha256 -Pkotlin.native.enabled=true resolveDependencies`.
* `resolveDependencies` task resolves dependencies for all platforms.
* Add OS-specific dependencies to `implicitDependencies` configuration.
* `local.properties` file can override Gradle command options like `kotlin.native.enabled`.
* Use Maven repository `https://redirector.kotlinlang.org/maven/bootstrap` for `-dev` versions.
* Kotlin is distributed under Apache License (Version 2.0).
* `./scripts/update-verification-metadata.sh` script updates verification metadata.
* `kotlin.native.enabled=true` is required for native-only dependencies to be added to verification metadata.

entries.title: Advanced Coroutines Patterns - Medium
entries.summary:
* Channels in Kotlin coroutines enable asynchronous data exchange between coroutines.
* Channel types: RendezvousChannel, ArrayChannel, UnlimitedChannel.
* RendezvousChannel: synchronous, send and receive operations suspend until both are ready.
* ArrayChannel: buffered, fixed capacity, send and receive operations suspend if buffer is full or empty.
* UnlimitedChannel: unbounded, unlimited capacity, send operations never suspend.
* Flows represent streams of asynchronous data that can be processed sequentially.
* Flow builders: `flow { … }`, `flowOf(…)` , `asFlow()`.
* Channels and flows are used for streaming data, producer-consumer relationships, and coordinating concurrent operations.
* `channel.close()` signals the end of data production in a channel.
* Use cases: streaming data from sensors or networks, task processing, coordinating concurrent operations.
* `flow { … }` creates a flow from a sequence of values emitted using `emit()`.
* `flowOf(…)` creates a flow from a fixed set of values.
* `asFlow()` converts data structures into flows.
* Flow operators: `map`, `filter`, `reduce`, `zip`, etc. for transforming data streams.
* `actor { … }` builder creates an actor for concurrent state management.
* Actors communicate via `send()` function and process messages in a separate coroutine.
* Use cases: processing data streams, reactive UI updates, asynchronous events, concurrent state management.
* Channels can connect flows, create data transformation pipelines, and manage a

* Gmail app sends large files by creating shareable Google Drive links for files over 25MB.

* Personalization options include favorite icons and color palettes.
* Google apps can be personalized with custom settings.

* Customization applies to Google apps and third-party apps.
* Gemini Nano v2 is a minimum requirement for certain features.
* Android offers expressive and customizable UI elements.

entries.title: Deployment guide: Manage Android devices in Microsoft Intu…
entries.summary:
* Create compliance policy for user and device groups in Microsoft Intune.
* Add actions for noncompliance when configuring device compliance policy.
* Create device-based or app-based Conditional Access policy to protect apps and services.
* Block access to apps that don't use modern authentication (OAuth2).
* Configure endpoint security features to manage device security and remediate issues.
* Enable mobile threat defense (MTD) connector for enrolled devices.
* Create MTD app protection policy to assess risks and limit device access.
* Create MTD device compliance policy to assess risk and limit corporate access.
* Configure device settings using device profiles in Microsoft Intune.
* Assign device profiles to groups to enable or disable settings and features on devices.
* Intune can remediate endpoint weaknesses identified by Defender's vulnerability management.
* Intune can wipe corporate data from managed apps.
* Intune allows selective removal of work-related data from a device.
* Intune provides enrollment tutorials for top tasks.
* Intune admin center is available for configuration.
* Intune supports enterprise mobility management (EMM) and app configuration for Slack.
* Intune has a separate deployment guide for iOS/iPadOS devices.
* Intune is integrated with Defender for vulnerability management.
* Intune provides a feedback mechanism for users to suggest fixes.
* Intune resources are updated regularly, with the last update on 2026-04-16.

entries.title: Multi-Module Clean Architecture for Android - Medium
entries.summary:
* Multi-module clean architecture is used for organizing Android app code.
* MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) pattern is applied for separating concerns.
* Kotlin is the preferred programming language for Android app development.
* Jetpack Compose is used for building user interfaces.
* Modularization helps in reducing app complexity and improving maintainability.
* Data, Domain, and Presentation are the main layers in clean architecture.
* Each module has its own build.gradle file for independent compilation.
* Dependency injection is used to manage dependencies between modules.
* Repository pattern is used for data access and manipulation.
* Use cases are defined in the Domain layer to encapsulate business logic.

entries.title: How to Build Scalable Android Apps Using Multi-Module Architecture
entries.summary:
* Multi-module architecture is used for building scalable Android apps
* Modularization helps reduce complexity and improve maintainability
* Android apps can be divided into feature modules and core modules
* Feature modules contain app-specific features and functionality
* Core modules contain shared code and libraries used across the app
* Gradle is used for building and managing multi-module Android projects
* Each module has its own build.gradle file for configuration
* Dependency injection is used to manage dependencies between modules
* Modular architecture allows for parallel development and testing
* Android Architecture Components can be used to implement multi-module architecture

entries.title: Mastering State Management in Flutter with Riverpod
entries.summary:
* Riverpod uses `ProviderContainer` to manage state.
* `addTearDown` is used to dispose of the container after testing.
* `container.read` is used to access providers and notifiers.
* Notifiers are used to update state, e.g., `addTodo` and `toggleTodo`.
* Providers can be overridden for testing purposes.
* Immutable state is recommended for use with Riverpod.
* Notifiers should contain business logic.
* Comprehensive testing is crucial for Riverpod state management.
* Riverpod supports type safety and testability.

entries.title: Use Phone Link to Sync Your Android or iPhone to Your Windows …
entries.summary:
* Phone Link requires iPhone with iOS 14 or higher, Windows 11 device, and Bluetooth connection.
* Phone Link is not compatible with iPad or MacOS.
* Device pairing uses Bluetooth and a QR code scanned by the iPhone's default camera.
* Alternative pairing method is available without a QR code, using a hyperlink on the iPhone.
* Required iPhone permissions include access to content and notifications.
* Compatible iPhone tasks include making calls, checking notifications, sending messages, and viewing contacts.
* Phone Link requires the latest version of the Phone Link app.
* Regional restrictions and device compatibility may vary.
* The Link to Windows app is available in the Apple Store for iPhone.
* Phone Link is available in the Microsoft Store for Windows PCs.
* Phone Link requires Bluetooth pairing between iPhone and PC to work.
* To unpair, remove device from Phone Link settings and "Forget this device" in Bluetooth settings.
* Customization options are available in Phone Link settings, including app notification permissions.
* iPhone must have iOS 14 or higher to use Phone Link.
* Phone Link is preinstalled on Windows 11 devices.
* Windows 11 is required to use Phone Link.
* Phone Link app has limitations on iOS, including no image/video sharing and group messaging.
* Messages are session-based and only come through when phone is connected to PC.
* Device compatibility may vary, and regional restrictions may apply.
* Phone Link is not available for iPad (iPadOS) or MacO

entries.title: Android Interview Series 2024 — Part 7 (Jetpack Compose)
entries.summary:
* `mutableStateOf` function returns a `MutableState` instance to hold and update values.
* Derived States in Compose create new states based on existing ones using `derivedStateOf`.
* `derivedStateOf` function computes derived values and only recomposes when the result changes.
* SideEffects in Jetpack Compose refer to state changes outside a composable function's scope.
* `LaunchedEffect` runs suspend functions within a composable's lifecycle.
* `DisposableEffect` manages resources that require setup and cleanup when a composable enters and exits composition.
* `rememberCoroutineScope` provides a coroutine scope tied to a composable's lifecycle.
* `rememberUpdatedState` keeps an updated reference to a value within long-lived composables.
* `LaunchedEffect` uses a `key` parameter to prevent unnecessary recomposition.
* `DisposableEffect` helps prevent memory leaks by cleaning up resources when no longer needed.
* Jetpack Compose uses `remember` to cache values across recompositions.
* `remember` prevents recalculation of unchanged values.
* `rememberSaveable` extends `remember` to preserve values across configuration changes.
* Configuration changes include screen rotations.
* `rememberSaveable` is used to maintain state across configuration changes.
* Optimisation techniques in Jetpack Compose include using `remember` and `rememberSaveable`.
* `remember` and `rememberSaveable` help improve performance by reducing unnecessary calculations.
* Values cached with `remember` are

entries.title: VPN.net – Hamachi by LogMeIn
entries.summary:
* LogMeIn Hamachi for Linux is a secure VPN client with command-line interface.
* Available as DEB and RPM packages for x86/x64 architectures.
* Compatible with Ubuntu (16.04LTS) and CentOS (7.2).
* ARM support available in beta version, requiring ARMv4T or better processor and tun/tap driver.
* Beta version not recommended for production environment.
* Download options: 32-bit, 64-bit, ARM, and ARM HF versions.
* Package names include logmein-hamachi with version and architecture suffixes.
* Installation files have .rpm, .deb, and .tgz extensions.
* Feedback for beta version can be sent to hamachi-feedback@logmein.com.

entries.title: How the Spurs held off the Knicks in Game 3 of the 2026 NBA ...
entries.summary:
* The Spurs won Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals 115-111 against the Knicks.
* Victor Wembanyama led the Spurs with 32 points.
* Jalen Brunson and OG Anunoby combined for 60 points for the Knicks.
* The Spurs used the bonus to their advantage, with every foul resulting in free points.
* The Spurs' defense was connected, making effective rotations to keep the Knicks from building momentum.
* Victor Wembanyama found his defensive rhythm in the fourth quarter.
* The Spurs' offense got deep into the paint to equalize the Knicks' advantage beyond the arc.
* The road team has won all three games in the series.
* The Knicks still lead the series 2-1 with Game 4 scheduled for Wednesday.
* The Spurs' win was a must-win to avoid falling behind 3-0 in the series.
* Spurs' defense improved with effective rotations to limit Knicks' offense.
* Victor Wembanyama found defensive rhythm in the 4th quarter.
* Knicks had 13 turnovers for 21 Spurs points, while Spurs had 8 turnovers for 7 Knicks points.
* Jalen Brunson led Knicks with 5 turnovers, averaging 4.3 turnovers per game in the series.
* Spurs used double drags and other actions to create spacing and free Wembanyama for lobs and dunks.
* Wembanyama was more intentional about fighting for post position.
* Spurs' adjustments allowed them to penetrate the Knicks' defense more effectively.
* Knicks' live-ball turnovers allowed Spurs to get out in transition and create advantages.
* De'Aaron Fox hit a signature pull-up to ice the game with 12.2
